title: Klaviyo vs Substack (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Klaviyo and Substack: pricing, features, and which email platform suits your needs best.
h1: Klaviyo vs Substack: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: Klaviyo and Substack are both email platforms, but they serve fundamentally different purposes. Klaviyo is a marketing automation powerhouse designed for e-commerce businesses, while Substack is a newsletter platform built for creators and writers to monetize directly from subscribers. This comparison helps you decide which tool aligns with your business model.

comparison_table.tool_b: Revenue-share (free or 10% cut of paid subscriptions)

comparison_table.tool_a: Predictive analytics, SMS, segmentation, Shopify/WooCommerce integration
comparison_table.tool_b: Paid subscriptions, built-in audience, podcast hosting, no subscriber limits

choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce store and need advanced marketing automation with SMS and segmentation
You require integrations with Shopify, WooCommerce, or other commerce platforms
You want predictive analytics and detailed customer behavior tracking for sales optimization
choose_b_if: You're a writer or creator wanting to monetize directly from your audience without payment processing
You want an unlimited subscriber base with no per-contact costs
You value simplicity and prefer a platform focused on content distribution over marketing tactics

faq.a: It depends on your use case. Klaviyo is superior for e-commerce marketing automation with advanced segmentation and analytics. Substack is better for independent creators and writers monetizing newsletters. They're designed for different audiences with different goals.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Klaviyo or Substack?
faq.a: Substack is typically cheaper for creators, as it's free until you earn money from paid subscriptions, then takes a 10% cut. Klaviyo has a free tier for up to 500 contacts, but paid plans start at $45/month—making it more expensive for businesses scaling beyond the free tier.

faq.q: Can I switch from Substack to Klaviyo?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your subscriber list from Substack and import it into Klaviyo. However, they serve different purposes—Substack handles monetization and content hosting, while Klaviyo focuses on marketing automation. You'd likely use them for different business objectives.
